metadata._llmHint: This file contains epic metadata and lightweight storyRefs. Full story content is at: stories/{storyId}.json (relative to this file). Do NOT add full story objects to this file — they belong in standalone story files.

content.title: AI Prioritization Engine
content.goal: Build an intelligent prioritization system that learns from team behavior and automatically ranks tasks by urgency, impact, and dependencies.
content.valueDelivered: Teams spend 60% less time on manual prioritization. AI suggestions improve sprint planning accuracy by 30%. Blocked tasks are surfaced proactively.

upstream.reason: AI engine requires task data, dependency graph, and historical velocity from Core Platform

downstream.reason: Focus Mode uses AI priority scores to filter and order the focus task queue

breakdown.description: ML model training pipeline, feature extraction, rule-based fallback
Priority distribution charts, velocity trends, anomaly detection
Capacity calculator, constraint solver, real-time forecast

implementationNotes: ML model trained on historical task completion data — needs minimum 50 completed tasks
Rule-based fallback uses deadline proximity, dependency blocking count, and manual priority
OpenAI API used for natural language priority inference — requires caching and rate limit handling
Priority scores recalculated on task mutation events from the Core Platform
content.acceptanceSummary: Epic is complete when AI scoring achieves > 80% accuracy vs. human-assigned priorities on golden dataset, fallback mode works without OpenAI, and sprint planning suggestions are accepted by EMs > 70% of the time.

functional.criterion: AI scoring completes within 2 seconds of task creation
Rule-based fallback activates when training data < 50 tasks

nonFunctional.criterion: Priority recalculation for 1000 tasks completes in < 30 seconds

security.criterion: Task data sent to OpenAI is anonymized (no PII)

business.measurement: Analytics tracking of accepted vs. modified suggestions

risks.risk: AI scoring may be inaccurate for small teams (< 5 members)
risks.impactDescription: Small teams may not generate enough training data for accurate ML scoring, leading to poor recommendations that erode trust
risks.mitigation: Minimum data threshold before enabling AI, transparent rule-based fallback

mitigationStrategies.strategy: Set minimum 50-task threshold before enabling ML scoring
Show confidence score alongside AI priority to set user expectations

risks.contingencyPlan: Allow teams to opt-out of AI scoring entirely and use manual priority only
triggers: User override rate exceeds 50% for AI-assigned priorities
NPS survey feedback mentions inaccurate priorities
risks.notes: Beta testing with 3 small teams to validate accuracy threshold

risks.risk: OpenAI API rate limits during peak usage
risks.impactDescription: Rate limiting during peak hours (9-11am) could delay priority scoring for all users simultaneously
risks.mitigation: Response caching, request batching, fallback to local scoring

mitigationStrategies.strategy: Implement 5-minute response cache for OpenAI API calls
Batch priority recalculation requests into single API calls
Local XGBoost model as fallback when API is unavailable

risks.contingencyPlan: Switch entirely to local XGBoost model if OpenAI costs exceed $500/month

items.item: AI scoring accuracy > 80% vs. human-assigned priorities (golden dataset)
Fallback mode works correctly when OpenAI is unavailable
Dashboard loads in < 2 seconds with 1000+ tasks

criteria: Fallback mode parity with manual scoring
No PII leaked to OpenAI API
